name: MITRE.LIN.T1531.RULE Account Access Removal
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

-a always,exit -S all -F dir=/etc/passwd -F perm=w -F uid!=0 -k siem-usr-access-rem
-a always,exit -S all -F dir=/etc/shadow -F perm=w -F uid!=0 -k siem-usr-access-rem

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-usr-access-rem
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1531.RULE Account Access Removal on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-usr-access-rem
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event name: MITRE.LIN.T1531.RULE Account Access Removal

Event description: Adversaries may interrupt availability of system and network resources by inhibiting access to accounts utilized by legitimate users.
See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1531/
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
